_comment: Fixtures for 17-loop-mode.yaml — schedule_wakeup multi-turn loop. Turn 1 ONLY schedules a wakeup and ends; the wakeup turn (turn 2) writes the output file and ends. Three authoring rules that bite here: (1) arg names must match the schedule_wakeup tool spec — delay_secs/reason/prompt, NOT the legacy seconds/message. A mismatch silently defaults delay_secs to the tool's unwrap_or(60), making the suite sleep 60s. (2) userMessage keys off the LATEST user message, so the wakeup turn (whose latest user msg is the schedule_wakeup prompt, not the goal) needs its own substring ('Continue') absent from the goal. (3) the loop shares the transcript across turns, so turn 2's first call already has hasToolResult=true from turn 1's schedule_wakeup result.

fixtures._comment: Turn 1, LLM call 0: agent schedules an immediate wakeup (goal is latest user msg, no tool result yet)

arguments.reason: continue to write the output file next turn
arguments.prompt: Continue: now write multi-loop.txt with content 'wakeup-done'

fixtures._comment: Turn 1, LLM call 1 (after schedule_wakeup result): agent ends turn 1 with a closing message (NoMoreToolCalls) so the loop can reschedule

response.content: Scheduled a wakeup; I'll write the file on the next turn.

fixtures._comment: Turn 2 (wakeup), LLM call 2: latest user msg is the wakeup prompt ('Continue…'), transcript already has turn 1's tool result, so hasToolResult=true
Turn 2, LLM call 3 (after write_file result): agent finishes without another wakeup, so the loop ends cleanly

response.content: Done! Wrote multi-loop.txt. No further wakeup needed.
